channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Blue Origin rocket explosion could set back NASA's plans to put astronauts on the moon
item.description: Engineers at Jeff Bezos' space company Blue Origin are trying to figure out why their massive New Glenn rocket exploded on its launch pad Thursday night.
item.content:encoded: Engineers at Jeff Bezos' space company Blue Origin are trying to figure out why their massive New Glenn rocket exploded on its launch pad Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Brendan Byrne

item.title: Rescuers are racing against time to reach 7 men trapped in a cave in Laos
item.description: In Laos, rescue efforts are underway to reach seven men who have been trapped inside a cave for over a week.
item.content:encoded: In Laos, rescue efforts are underway to reach seven men who have been trapped inside a cave for over a week.
item.dc:creator: Jan Camenzind Broomby

item.title: A Kenyan court has temporarily halted U.S. plans to open an Ebola quarantine center
item.description: A Kenyan court has temporarily halted the opening of a U.S. Ebola quarantine center. The move follows protests over infection risks and claims that the country could be used as a "containment colony."
item.content:encoded: A Kenyan court has temporarily halted the opening of a U.S. Ebola quarantine center. The move follows protests over infection risks and claims that the country could be used as a "containment colony."
item.dc:creator: Michael Kaloki

item.title: How primaries are shaping Congress
item.description: The Primary used to be just the first step in the electoral process — now the primary has transformed, in many places into the moment when the election is all but decided. It's shaping Congress.
item.content:encoded: The Primary used to be just the first step in the electoral process — now the primary has transformed, in many places into the moment when the election is all but decided. It's shaping Congress.
item.dc:creator: Eric McDaniel

item.title: Drones are changing the face of warfare, including battlefield medicine
item.description: There have been hundreds of thousands of casualties on both sides of the war in Ukraine, and by some estimates more than 80% are now caused by drones. It's changed the nature of battlefield medicine.
item.content:encoded: There have been hundreds of thousands of casualties on both sides of the war in Ukraine, and by some estimates more than 80% are now caused by drones. It's changed the nature of battlefield medicine.
item.dc:creator: Jay Price

item.title: New bear cubs may lead Yellowstone grizzlies off of Endangered Species list
item.description: Conservationists are celebrating the first Yellowstone-area grizzly cub born with DNA from outside that genetically isolated population. It's a milestone for the species.
item.content:encoded: Conservationists are celebrating the first Yellowstone-area grizzly cub born with DNA from outside that genetically isolated population. It's a milestone for the species.
item.dc:creator: Nick Mott

item.title: Fan favorite Boards of Canada returns with first new album in 13 years
item.description: The band Boards of Canada has a large and dedicated community of fans. Their 13-year-long wait for a new album is now over.
item.content:encoded: The band Boards of Canada has a large and dedicated community of fans. Their 13-year-long wait for a new album is now over.
item.dc:creator: Juana Summers

item.title: Questioned by House lawmakers, Bondi defends her handling of Epstein files
item.description: Former Attorney General Pam Bondi was questioned by House lawmakers Friday about her handling of the release of files in the case of convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.
item.content:encoded: Former Attorney General Pam Bondi was questioned by House lawmakers Friday about her handling of the release of files in the case of convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: Common myths about gut health
item.description: Marketers sell all kinds of tests and supplements to fix your gut health. But is any of this actually worth spending money on?
item.content:encoded: Marketers sell all kinds of tests and supplements to fix your gut health. But is any of this actually worth spending money on?
item.dc:creator: Will Stone

item.title: President Trump's push to be on a new $250 bill
item.description: NPR's Sacha Pfeiffer talks with financial historian Brendan Greeley about President Trump's push to be featured on a new $250 bill.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Sacha Pfeiffer talks with financial historian Brendan Greeley about President Trump's push to be featured on a new $250 bill.
item.dc:creator: Kathryn Fink
